context: Tests whether the agent breaks the circular dependency by introducing an interface/abstraction or reorganizing responsibilities, rather than just suppressing the error.

checklist.name: Circular import eliminated
checklist.description: The refactored code has no import cycle: neither module directly imports the other

checklist.name: Interface or event-based decoupling used
checklist.description: The solution introduces an interface (e.g., IPaymentHistoryProvider) or event/callback mechanism rather than keeping direct class coupling

checklist.name: REFACTOR.md identifies root cause
checklist.description: REFACTOR.md explains that the cycle arose because two modules depend on each other's concrete classes

checklist.name: REFACTOR.md explains resolution approach
checklist.description: REFACTOR.md describes the chosen approach (interface extraction, dependency inversion, shared contract module, or event decoupling)

checklist.name: Both services remain functional
checklist.description: PaymentService can still trigger payment confirmation and NotificationService can still access payment history (behavior preserved)
